query: GSK Arexvy FDA prescribing information RSV vaccine 2025 updated safety efficacy labeling

mako.raw:
{"query":"GSK Arexvy FDA prescribing information RSV vaccine 2025 updated safety efficacy labeling","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1673,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://us.gsk.com/en-us/media/press-releases/fda-accepts-application-to-review-expanded-use-of-gsk-s-rsv-vaccine-arexvy/","title":"US FDA accepts application to review expanded use of GSK’s RSV vaccine, Arexvy, for adults 18-49 at increased risk | GSK US","content":"GSK plc (LSE/NYSE: GSK) today announced that the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has accepted for review an application to extend the indication of Arexvy (Respiratory Syncytial Virus Vaccine, Adjuvanted) to adults aged 18-49 who are at increased risk.
GSK is continuing to seek expanded indications for its RSV vaccine in other geographies including in the European Economic Area and Japan.
The FDA has approved GSK’s RSV vaccine for the prevention of lower respiratory tract disease (LRTD) caused by respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) in individuals 60 years of age and older, and those aged 50-59 who are at increased risk.
The AS01 adjuvant system, owned by GSK, includes the QS-21 adjuvant, licensed by Antigenics, a wholly owned subsidiary of Agenus.
The results of a postmarketing observational study suggest an increased risk of Guillain-Barré syndrome during the 42 days following vaccination with AREXVY
GSK is a global biopharma company with a purpose to unite science, technology, and talent to get ahead of disease together.
GSK cautions investors that any forward-looking statements or projections made by GSK, including those made in this announcement, are subject to risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results to differ materially from those projected."},{"url":"https://www.fda.gov/vaccines-blood-biologics/arexvy","title":"AREXVY | FDA","content":"Refer to Section 1.1 of the Clinical Review Memo for information about participation in the clinical trials and any analysis of demographic subgroup outcomes that is notable."},{"url":"https://us.gsk.com/en-us/media/press-releases/gsk-s-rsv-vaccine-arexvy-approved-in-us-for-expanded-age-indication-in-adults-aged-18-49-years-at-increased-risk/","title":"GSK’s RSV vaccine, AREXVY, approved in US for expanded age indication in adults aged 18–49 years at increased risk | GSK US","content":"The FDA’s decision was supported by data from a Phase IIIb trial (NCT06389487) demonstrating a non-inferior immune response compared to adults aged 60 years and above.3 Vaccine efficacy was demonstrated in the earlier Phase III trial (NCT04886596).4 The safety profile was consistent with findings from the broader Phase III program that supported the initial US approval, with the most common adverse events being injection site pain, fatigue, myalgia, headache, and arthralgia within four days of vaccination.3
In the European Economic Area it is approved for adults aged 18 years and older.
STIMULON is a trademark of SaponiQx Inc., a subsidiary of Agenus.
NCT06389487 is a Phase IIIb, open-label multi-country immunogenicity trial to evaluate the non-inferiority of the immune response and evaluate safety in participants aged 18–49 at increased risk for lower respiratory tract disease (LRTD) caused by RSV (n=426), compared to participants aged 60 years and above (n=429) after a single dose of GSK’s RSV vaccine.
Such factors include, but are not limited to, those described in the “Risk Factors” section in GSK’s Annual Report on Form 20-F for 2025.
Last accessed: March 2026.
Respiratory syncytial virus infection in elderly and high-risk adults, in New Engl J Med 2005; 352:1749-59. doi: 10.1056/NEJMoa043951.
Economic burden of respiratory syncytial virus infection in adults: a systematic literature review."},{"url":"https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2025.1624007/full","title":"a systematic review of Arexvy, Abrysvo, and mResvia","content":"by TA Alandijany · 2025 · Cited by 22 — Arexvy reduced RSV-related hospitalizations in older adults by 60–65% (95% CI: 56–66%); Abrysvo showed 58–63% efficacy in older adults and 68–72% protection ...Read more"}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"GSK Arexvy FDA prescribing information RSV vaccine 2025 updated safety efficacy labeling","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.pharmexec.com/view/fda-mandates-updates-prescribing-information-rsv-vaccines-abrysvo-arexvy-increased-guillain-barr-syndrome-risk","title":"FDA Mandates Updates to Prescribing Information for RSV ...","content":"Jan 9, 2025 — Arexvy will now be required to come with labeling that includes a warning about a potential increased risk of Guillain-Barré Syndrome.","score":0.8945781,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.optometryadvisor.com/news/fda-adds-warning-label-to-rsv-vaccines-abrysvo-and-arexvy","title":"FDA Adds Warning Label to RSV Vaccines Abrysvo and Arexvy - Optometry Advisor","content":"# FDA Adds Warning Label to RSV Vaccines Abrysvo and Arexvy. HealthDay News — The U.S. Food and Drug Administration now requires and has approved safety labeling changes to the prescribing information for the Abrysvo (Pfizer) and Arexvy (GlaxoSmithKline) respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) vaccines to include warnings and precautions about the risk for Guillain-Barré syndrome (GBS). Arexvy has been approved for the prevention of lower respiratory tract disease caused by RSV in all individuals 60 years of age and older and for those aged 50 to 59 years at increased risk. Abrysvo has been approved for the prevention of lower respiratory tract disease caused by RSV in all individuals 60 years of age and older, as well as individuals aged 18 to 59 years at increased risk. Abrysvo is also approved for pregnant individuals at 32 through 36 weeks of gestational age for the prevention of lower respiratory tract disease caused by RSV in infants from birth through 6 months of age.","score":0.796562,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.fda.gov/files/vaccines%2C%20blood%20%26%20biologics/published/Package-Insert-AREXVY.pdf","title":"Package Insert - AREXVY","content":"AREXVY (Respiratory Syncytial Virus Vaccine, Adjuvanted) for injectable suspension, for intramuscular use Initial U.S. Approval: 2023 ----------------------------- RECENT MAJOR CHANGES --------------------------- Indications and Usage (1) Dosage and Administration (2) 3/2026 4/2026 ----------------------------- INDICATIONS AND USAGE ---------------------------- AREXVY is a vaccine indicated for active immunization for the prevention of lower respiratory tract disease (LRTD) caused by respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) in: • individuals 60 years of age and older; (1) • individuals 18 through 59 years of age who are at increased risk for LRTD caused by RSV.","score":0.74785584,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.fda.gov/vaccines-blood-biologics/safety-availability-biologics/fda-requires-guillain-barre-syndrome-gbs-warning-prescribing-information-rsv-vaccines-abrysvo-and","title":"FDA Requires Guillain-Barré Syndrome (GBS) Warning in the Prescribing Information for RSV Vaccines Abrysvo and Arexvy | FDA","content":"FDA conducted a postmarketing observational study that assessed the risk of GBS following vaccination with Abrysvo and Arexvy. Based on FDA’s evaluation of data from clinical trials, reports to the Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS), and the postmarketing study, FDA has determined that the overall body of evidence suggests increased risks of GBS with Abrysvo and Arexvy, but that available evidence is insufficient to establish a causal relationship. The approval of Abrysvo in individuals 18 through 59 years of age who are at increased risk for LRTD caused by RSV included a requirement by FDA for the manufacturer to conduct a study to evaluate GBS in this population. FDA has required and approved safety labeling changes to the Prescribing Information for Abrysvo and Arexvy based on the totality of data from clinical trials, reports to VAERS, and the results of self-controlled case series analyses in an observational study conducted by FDA that suggest increased risks of GBS with Abrysvo and Arexvy.","score":0.7440808,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://us.gsk.com/en-us/media/press-releases/fda-accepts-application-to-review-expanded-use-of-gsk-s-rsv-vaccine-arexvy","title":"US FDA accepts application to review expanded use of GSK’s RSV vaccine, Arexvy, for adults 18-49 at increased risk | GSK US","content":"# US FDA accepts application to review expanded use of GSK’s RSV vaccine, Arexvy, for adults 18-49 at increased risk. GSK’s RSV vaccine is approved in the US for the prevention of lower respiratory tract disease (LRTD) caused by RSV in adults aged 60 and older, and for those aged 50-59 years who are at increased risk for LRTD caused by RSV. This regulatory submission is supported by a phase IIIb trial evaluating immune response and safety in adults aged 18-49 at increased risk compared to adults aged 60 and above.6 The safety and reactogenicity data were consistent with results from the phase III program that supported the initial approval of the vaccine in the US. The FDA has approved GSK’s RSV vaccine for the prevention of lower respiratory tract disease (LRTD) caused by respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) in individuals 60 years of age and older, and those aged 50-59 who are at increased risk.","score":0.60666215,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.84,"request_id":"6c1c40ae-fce4-4831-a115-70bbc5c31cfe"}
